# California Waiver Template
## Exact Text Generated by ObraKit

Our California template generates a PDF that matches the statutory forms in Cal. Civ. Code SS 8132-8138.

---

## Template Structure

### Title (13pt, centered, uppercase)
```
CONDITIONAL WAIVER AND RELEASE ON PROGRESS PAYMENT
```
(Changes based on waiver type: conditional/unconditional, progress/final)

### Statute Reference (8pt, centered, gray)
```
Cal. Civ. Code SS 8132-8138
```

### NOTICE BOX (13pt bold, 2px border)

**For Conditional Waivers (both progress and final):**
```
NOTICE: THIS DOCUMENT WAIVES THE CLAIMANT'S LIEN, STOP PAYMENT NOTICE,
AND PAYMENT BOND RIGHTS EFFECTIVE ON RECEIPT OF PAYMENT. A PERSON SHOULD
NOT RELY ON THIS DOCUMENT UNLESS SATISFIED THAT THE CLAIMANT HAS RECEIVED
PAYMENT.
```

**For Unconditional Waivers (both progress and final):**
```
NOTICE: THIS DOCUMENT WAIVES AND RELEASES LIEN, STOP PAYMENT NOTICE, AND
PAYMENT BOND RIGHTS UNCONDITIONALLY AND STATES THAT YOU HAVE BEEN PAID FOR
GIVING UP THOSE RIGHTS. THIS DOCUMENT IS ENFORCEABLE AGAINST YOU IF YOU
SIGN IT, EVEN IF YOU HAVE NOT BEEN PAID. IF YOU HAVE NOT BEEN PAID, USE A
CONDITIONAL WAIVER AND RELEASE FORM.
```

**QUESTION:** Does the NOTICE text exactly match the current statute? California requires it be in "type at least as large as the largest type on the form." Our NOTICE is 13pt and our title is also 13pt. Is this compliant?

### Identifying Information (10pt)
```
Name of Claimant:       [company name]
Name of Customer:       [GC name]
Job Location:           [project address, city, state, zip]
Owner:                  [property owner name]
Through Date:           [date]
```

### Conditional Body (only for conditional waivers)
```
This document waives and releases lien, stop payment notice, and payment
bond rights through the Through Date of this document but only to the
extent of payment received, conditioned on receipt of the following check:

Maker of Check:         [check maker name]
Amount of Check:        [dollar amount]
```

### Unconditional Progress Body
```
The claimant has been paid and has received a progress payment in the sum
of $[amount] for labor, services, equipment, or material furnished to the
jobsite identified above, and gives up any right to a mechanic's lien,
stop payment notice, and any right to make a claim against a labor and
material bond on the job to the extent of the amount paid.
```

### Unconditional Final Body
```
The claimant has been paid in full for all labor, services, equipment, or
material furnished to the jobsite identified above and gives up any right
to a mechanic's lien, stop payment notice, and any right to make a claim
against a labor and material bond on the job.
```

### Exceptions
```
Exceptions:             [user-provided text or "None"]
```

### Signature Area
```
_________________________          _________________________
Claimant's Signature               Date

Claimant's Title: _______________
```

### Footer (7pt, gray)
```
Generated by ObraKit.ai
```
(Only shown for free-tier users)

---

## Questions for Counsel

1. Is our NOTICE language an exact match to Cal. Civ. Code SS 8132 (conditional progress), S 8134 (unconditional progress), S 8136 (conditional final), S 8138 (unconditional final)?

2. California requires forms be in "substantially" the form provided. Does our template structure (field order, labels, body language) substantially conform?

3. We use the same NOTICE text for both progress and final conditional waivers, and the same NOTICE for both progress and final unconditional waivers. Is this correct per statute?

4. We include a "Generated by ObraKit.ai" footer on free-tier waivers. Does this addition violate the "substantially the form" requirement?

5. Should the "Exceptions" field be included on all form types, or only certain ones per the statute?
